SMALL CLAIMS HEARING OF JUN-05-2009 IS OFF CALENDAR. DUPLICATE ENTRY.

REMOVED FROM SMALL CLAIMS - CLAIM - TRIAL DEPARTMENT CALENDAR ON JUN-05-2009 IN DEPT. 501. DEFENDANT MULLIS, MICHELLE DOES NOT OWE PLAINTIFF FULLER, MICHELLE ANY MONEY ON PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. (SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT).

THE COURT HAS ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF FULLER, MICHELLE IS NOT OWED ANY MONEY BY DEFENDANT MULLIS, MICHELE THE COURT FOUND PLAINTIFF BREACHED THE WARRANTY OF HABITABILITY & QUIET ENJOYMENT GIVING DEFENDANT A GOOD CAUSE TO VACATE THE PREMISES AND TERMINATE TENANCY AS OF JUNE 30, 2008. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY DEFENDANT $1,397.50 THE REMAINDER OF THE SECURITY DEPOSIT. STAY OF EXECUTION 30 DAYS PLAINTIFF'S AND DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RECEIVED

calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Sep-11-2009 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Michelle Fuller, Appellant/Cross Defendant, appears in pro per
Michelle Mullis, Respondent/Cross Complainant, appear in pro per

This cause comes on regularly for trial de novo. Both parties have signed the Stipulation to Use Judge Pro Tem. The clerk administers an Oath to both parties.

Parties present their respective cases by testimony and documentary evidence.

At the conclusion of the hearing, the matter is taken under submission. No evidence is retained.

JPT David Edward May, Clerk Clark Banayad, not reported (525)

SMALL CLAIMS - CLAIM - TRIAL COURT CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-08-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Regular Small Claims Court Trial(Landlord Tenant Dispute)

Appearance: Ms. Michelle Fuller-Plaintiff
Ms. Michelle Mullis-Defendant

Ruling; Argued and the court took the matter under submission. Plaintiff presents to the court a copy of the lease contract & E-mails communications and several documents submitted to the court towards the end of the hearing placed in a plaintiff brown Manila envelope. Defendant presents the following copy; Notice to plaintiff reason why she is moving out & hotel receipt.Exhibits to be returned when judgment is rendered and several documents placed in Defendant's gry envelope. (Judge R. Quidachay, Clerk R. Pascual)
